what: Low, medium and high cloud description
title: Code Tables
text: Important Note: original Code Table use “/” instead “10”.
code: # From Code Table 0513 low_cloud_codes <- c(   "0" = "No CL clouds",   "1" = "Cumulus humilis or cumulus fractus other than of bad weather,* or both",   "2" = "Cumulus mediocris or congestus, with or without cumulus of species fractus or humilis or stratocumulus, all having their bases at the same level",   "3" = "Cumulonimbus calvus, with or without cumulus, stratocumulus or Stratus",   "4" = "Stratocumulus cumulogenitus",   "5" = "Stratocumulus other than stratocumulus cumulogenitus",   "6" = "Stratus nebulosus or Stratus fractus other than of bad weather,* or both",   "7" = "Stratus fractus or cumulus fractus of bad weather,* or both (pannus), usually below altostratus or nimbostratus",   "8" = "Cumulus and stratocumulus other than stratocumulus cumulogenitus, with bases at different levels",   "9" = "Cumulonimbus capillatus (often with an anvil), with or without cumulonimbus calvus, cumulus, stratocumulus, Stratus or pannus",   "10" = "CL clouds invisible owing to darkness, fog, blowing dust or sand, or other similar phenomena" )  # From Code Table 0515 medium_cloud_codes <- c(   "0" = "No CM clouds",   "1" = "Altostratus translucidus",   "2" = "Altostratus opacus or nimbostratus",   "3" = "Altocumulus translucidus at a single level",   "4" = "Patches (often lenticular) of altocumulus translucidus, continually changing and occurring at one or more levels",   "5" = "Altocumulus translucidus in bands, or one or more layers of altocumulus translucidus or opacus, progressively invading the sky; these altocumulus clouds generally thicken as a whole",   "6" = "Altocumulus cumulogenitus (or cumulonimbogenitus)",   "7" = "Altocumulus translucidus or opacus in two or more layers, or altocumulus opacus in a single layer, not progressively invading the sky, or altocumulus with altostratus or nimbostratus",   "8" = "Altocumulus castellanus or floccus",   "9" = "Altocumulus of a chaotic sky, generally at several levels",   "10" = "CM clouds invisible owing to darkness, fog, blowing dust or sand, or other similar phenomena, or because of continuous layer of lower clouds" )  # From Code Table 0509 high_cloud_codes <- c(   "0" = "No CH clouds",   "1" = "Cirrus fibratus, sometimes uncinus, not progressively invading the sky",   "2" = "Cirrus spissatus, in patches or entangled sheaves, which usually do not increase and sometimes seem to be the remains of the upper part of a cumulonimbus; or cirrus castellanus or floccus",   "3" = "Cirrus spissatus cumulonimbogenitus",   "4" = "Cirrus uncinus or fibratus, or both, progressively invading the sky; they generally thicken as a whole",   "5" = "Cirrus (often in bands) and cirrostratus, or cirrostratus alone, progressively invading the sky; they generally thicken as a whole, but the continuous veil does not reach 45 degrees above the horizon",   "6" = "Cirrus (often in bands) and cirrostratus, or cirrostratus alone, progressively invading the sky; they generally thicken as a whole; the continuous veil extends more than 45 degrees above the horizon, without the sky being totally covered",   "7" = "Cirrostratus covering the whole sky",   "8" = "Cirrostratus not progressively invading the sky and not entirely covering it",   "9" = "Cirrocumulus alone, or cirrocumulus predominant among the CH clouds",   "10" = "CH clouds invisible owing to darkness, fog, blowing dust or sand, or other similar phenomena, or because of a continuous layer of lower clouds" )

title: Introducing synopR
text: show_synop_data() requires character vector data frame column element SYNOP string. meteorological parameter isn’t present SYNOP messages, can set remove_empty_cols = TRUE remove extra columns. optional wmo_identifier argument offers significant advantage: allows automatic filtering case data contains messages stations. following example uses vector two messages simplicity, working thousands SYNOP strings multiple stations, built-filtering becomes extremely convenient. good practice check SYNOP messages non-standard structures. check_synop() function designed handle . make sure every message starts “AAXX” ends “=”, contain invalid characters (valid characters removing “AAXX” “=” digits 0-9, ‘/’ ‘NIL’), verifies groups consist 5 digits (except section identifiers ‘333’ ‘555’). check_synop() function accepts either string vector specific data frame column containing SYNOP strings. data frame multiple columns —SYNOP column explicitly specified— accepted data frame direct output parse_ogimet(). far, messages correct structure (even NIL ones). Now, let’s see happens don’t. check_synop() returns tibble first column indicates whether SYNOP valid (TRUE) (FALSE), second column describes specific error found. example: first SYNOP correct. second, missing space groups 6 8 Section 1. third, group 2 Section 3 contains 4 digits. fourth message missing “=” terminator (remember SYNOP messages must always start “AAXX” end “=”). fifth simply SYNOP string .
code: library(synopR) data_input_vector <- c("AAXX 04003 87736 32965 00000 10204 20106 39982 40074 5//// 333 10266 20158 555 64169 65090 =",                        "AAXX 01094 87736 NIL=",                        "AAXX 03183 87736 32965 12708 10254 20052 30005 40098 5//// 80005 333 56000 81270 =")  my_data <- show_synop_data(data_input_vector, wmo_identifier = '87736')  print(my_data) #> # A tibble: 3 × 21 #>   wmo_id   Day  Hour Cloud_base_height Visibility Total_cloud_cover #>   <chr>  <dbl> <dbl>             <dbl>      <dbl>             <dbl> #> 1 87736      4     0                 9         65                 0 #> 2 87736      1     9                NA         NA                NA #> 3 87736      3    18                 9         65                 1 #> # ℹ 15 more variables: Wind_direction <dbl>, Wind_speed <dbl>, #> #   Wind_speed_unit <chr>, Air_temperature <dbl>, Dew_point <dbl>, #> #   Relative_humidity <dbl>, Station_pressure <dbl>, MSLP_GH <dbl>, #> #   Cloud_amount_Nh <dbl>, Low_clouds_CL <dbl>, Medium_clouds_CM <dbl>, #> #   High_clouds_CH <dbl>, Max_temperature <dbl>, Min_temperature <dbl>, #> #   Cloud_drift_direction <chr> library(synopR) # Messages from 87736 and 87016 mixed_synop <- c("AAXX 01183 87736 12465 20000 10326 20215 39974 40064 5//// 60001 82100 333 56600 82818=",                  "AAXX 04033 87016 41460 83208 10200 20194 39712 40114 50003 70292 888// 333 56699 82810 88615="                  )  colorado_data <- show_synop_data(mixed_synop, wmo_identifier = '87736', remove_empty_cols = TRUE) #> Warning in show_synop_data(mixed_synop, wmo_identifier = "87736", #> remove_empty_cols = TRUE): 1 message(s) do not contain the identifier '87736' #> and will be discarded. knitr::kable(t(colorado_data)) library(synopR)  my_df <- data.frame(syn = c("AAXX 01183 87736 12465 20000 10326 20215 39974 40064 5//// 60001 82100 333 56600 82818=",                             "AAXX 01183 87736 12465 20000 10326 20215 39974 40064 5//// 60001 82100 333 56600 82818="),                     second_column = c(5,7))  check_synop(my_df) # Bad #> Error: #> ℹ In index: 1. #> Caused by error in `startsWith()`: #> ! non-character object(s)  check_synop(my_df$syn) # Good #> # A tibble: 2 × 2 #>   is_valid error_log #>   <lgl>    <chr>     #> 1 TRUE     ""        #> 2 TRUE     "" library(synopR)  check_synop(c("AAXX 01183 87736 12465 20000 10326 20215 39974 40064 5//// 60001 82100 333 56600 82818=",               "AAXX 01183 87736 12465 20000 10326 20215 39974 40064 5//// 6000182100 333 56600 82818=",               "AAXX 01183 87736 12465 20000 10326 2021 39974 40064 5//// 60001 82100 333 56600 82818=",               "AAXX 01183 87736 12465 20000 10326 20215 39974 40064 5//// 60001 82100 333 56600 82818",               "Not a synop message=")) #> # A tibble: 5 × 2 #>   is_valid error_log                                               #>   <lgl>    <chr>                                                   #> 1 TRUE     ""                                                      #> 2 FALSE    "Invalid groups: 6000182100"                            #> 3 FALSE    "Invalid groups: 2021"                                  #> 4 FALSE    "Missing '=' terminator"                                #> 5 FALSE    "Missing AAXX | Invalid groups: Not, a, synop, message"

title: Introducing synopR
text: following SYNOP messages retrieved Ogimet Rio Colorado station, Argentina (WMO identifier: 87736). observe “pure” SYNOP strings; include prefix added Ogimet specifies station ID (87736) along date time observation. However, issue, can use parse_ogimet() function. tool specifically designed separate aggregates raw SYNOP message processing.
code: library(synopR)  data_input <- data.frame(synops = c("87736,2026,02,01,03,00,AAXX 01034 87736 NIL=",                                     "87736,2026,02,01,06,00,AAXX 01064 87736 NIL=",                                     "87736,2026,02,01,09,00,AAXX 01094 87736 NIL=",                                     "87736,2026,02,01,12,00,AAXX 01123 87736 12965 31808 10240 20210 39992 40082 5//// 60104 82075 333 10282 20216 56055 82360=",                                     "87736,2026,02,01,15,00,AAXX 01154 87736 NIL=",                                     "87736,2026,02,01,18,00,AAXX 01183 87736 12465 20000 10326 20215 39974 40064 5//// 60001 82100 333 56600 82818=",                                     "87736,2026,02,01,21,00,AAXX 01214 87736 NIL="))  # Note that `parse_ogimet(data_input)` is incorrect data_from_ogimet <- parse_ogimet(data_input$synops)   print(data_from_ogimet) #> # A tibble: 7 × 5 #>    Year Month Day_Ogimet Hour_Ogimet Raw_synop                                   #>   <dbl> <dbl>      <dbl>       <dbl> <chr>                                       #> 1  2026     2          1           3 AAXX 01034 87736 NIL=                       #> 2  2026     2          1           6 AAXX 01064 87736 NIL=                       #> 3  2026     2          1           9 AAXX 01094 87736 NIL=                       #> 4  2026     2          1          12 AAXX 01123 87736 12965 31808 10240 20210 3… #> 5  2026     2          1          15 AAXX 01154 87736 NIL=                       #> 6  2026     2          1          18 AAXX 01183 87736 12465 20000 10326 20215 3… #> 7  2026     2          1          21 AAXX 01214 87736 NIL=  # A 'Year' column is included! parse_ogimet(data_input$synops) |> show_synop_data(wmo_identifier = 87736, remove_empty_cols = TRUE) #> # A tibble: 7 × 25 #>   wmo_id  Year Month   Day  Hour Cloud_base_height Visibility Total_cloud_cover #>   <chr>  <dbl> <dbl> <dbl> <dbl>             <dbl>      <dbl>             <dbl> #> 1 87736   2026     2     1     3                NA         NA                NA #> 2 87736   2026     2     1     6                NA         NA                NA #> 3 87736   2026     2     1     9                NA         NA                NA #> 4 87736   2026     2     1    12                 9         65                 3 #> 5 87736   2026     2     1    15                NA         NA                NA #> 6 87736   2026     2     1    18                 4         65                 2 #> 7 87736   2026     2     1    21                NA         NA                NA #> # ℹ 17 more variables: Wind_direction <dbl>, Wind_speed <dbl>, #> #   Wind_speed_unit <chr>, Air_temperature <dbl>, Dew_point <dbl>, #> #   Relative_humidity <dbl>, Station_pressure <dbl>, MSLP_GH <dbl>, #> #   Precipitation_S1 <dbl>, Precip_period_S1 <dbl>, Cloud_amount_Nh <dbl>, #> #   Low_clouds_CL <dbl>, Medium_clouds_CM <dbl>, High_clouds_CH <dbl>, #> #   Max_temperature <dbl>, Min_temperature <dbl>, Cloud_drift_direction <chr>

title: Introducing synopR
text: validity SYNOP string doesn’t mean content correct. quality-control derived data included. Data post-processing . Group 555 (reserved national distribution) currently ignored, content varies country. However, future versions synopR may include functions extract data section based user requirements. support sections 222 y 444. show_synop_data() incorrectly decode message.

title: Introducing synopR
text: following meteorological parameters completely decoded, produce strictly numeric vector, output long: Horizontal visibility VV Lowest cloud base height h Cloud cover N Nh, can directly interpreted oktas (octaves), except ’s 9, means sky visible due fog meteorological phenomenon Present past weather ww, W1, W2, cloud-related Cl, Cm Ch. ground-related E E' However, Code tables available section “Code Tables” direct conversions! also aware : Wind direction = 99 means “variable wind direction” Wind speed greater 99 units (m/s knots) supported (final result 99), ’s expected won’t break function group 2 section 1 informs relative humidity instead dew point, final value Dew_point column NA geopotential height, pressure levels 850, 700 500 hPa supported (others pressure levels result NA) Groups 5 9 section 1 ignored Imperceptible precipitation, codified 990, considered 0.01 (mm), can distinguished 0 value cloud description “/” (clouds visible) mapped 10 Snow depth sss assumed 1 cm 996 cm. ‘997’ means ‘less 0.5 cm’, 998 “Snow cover, continuous” 999 “Measurement impossible inaccurate” Groups 5 (including 55, 56, 57, etc…), 7, 8 9 section 3 ignored

title: Introducción a synopR
text: Todo lo que necesita show_synop_data() es un string vector o la columna de un dataframe, donde cada elemento sea un mensaje SYNOP. Si un parámetro meteorológico está presente en ninguno de los mensajes, puedes usar el argumento remove_empty_cols = TRUE para remover las columnas adicionales que se generan. El argumento opcional wmo_identifier ofrece una ventaja significativa: permite filtrar en caso de que tu archivo contenga mensajes de otras estaciones. El siguiente ejemplo, por cuestiones de simplicidad, sólo utiliza dos mensajes, pero si estás trabajando con cientos de SYNOP, la posibilidad de hacer un filtro desde esta función se vuelve extremadamente conveniente. Es una buena práctica chequear los mensajes en busca de estructuras estandarizadas. La función check_synop() se encarga de esto. Se asegurará que cada mensaje comience con “AAXX” y termine con “=”, que contenga caracteres inválidos (los caracteres válidos, luego de remover “AAXX” y “=” son los números del 0 9, ‘/’ y ‘NIL’), y verifica que todos los grupos contengan 5 dígitos (excepto para los identificadores ‘333’ y ‘555’). La función check_synop() acepta un string vector o la columna de un data frame que específicamente contiene los mensajes SYNOP. Un data frame de varias columnas (cuando la columna con los SYNOP se especifica explícitamente) será aceptado ** si y solo si ese data frame es el resultado directo de** parse_ogimet(). Hasta el momento, todos nuestros mensajes tienen una estructura correcta (incluso los NIL). Ahora, veamos que sucede cuando . check_synop() devuelve un tibble donde la primera columna indica si el SYNOP es válido (TRUE) o (FALSO), y una segunda columna, con el detalle del error encontrado. En nuestro ejemplo: El primer SYNOP es correcto. Al segundo le falta un espacio entre el grupo 6 y el grupo 8 en la sección 1 En el tercero, el grupo 2 de la sección 3 contiene sólamente 4 cifras Al cuarto mensaje le falta la terminación “=” (los SYNOP deben empezar con “AAXX” y terminar con “=”) El quinto es cualquier cosa menos un SYNIP
code: library(synopR)  data_input_vector <- c("AAXX 04003 87736 32965 00000 10204 20106 39982 40074 5//// 333 10266 20158 555 64169 65090 =",                        "AAXX 01094 87736 NIL=",                        "AAXX 03183 87736 32965 12708 10254 20052 30005 40098 5//// 80005 333 56000 81270 =")  my_data <- show_synop_data(data_input_vector, wmo_identifier = '87736')  print(my_data) #> # A tibble: 3 × 21 #>   wmo_id   Day  Hour Cloud_base_height Visibility Total_cloud_cover #>   <chr>  <dbl> <dbl>             <dbl>      <dbl>             <dbl> #> 1 87736      4     0                 9         65                 0 #> 2 87736      1     9                NA         NA                NA #> 3 87736      3    18                 9         65                 1 #> # ℹ 15 more variables: Wind_direction <dbl>, Wind_speed <dbl>, #> #   Wind_speed_unit <chr>, Air_temperature <dbl>, Dew_point <dbl>, #> #   Relative_humidity <dbl>, Station_pressure <dbl>, MSLP_GH <dbl>, #> #   Cloud_amount_Nh <dbl>, Low_clouds_CL <dbl>, Medium_clouds_CM <dbl>, #> #   High_clouds_CH <dbl>, Max_temperature <dbl>, Min_temperature <dbl>, #> #   Cloud_drift_direction <chr> library(synopR) # Messages from 87736 and 87016 mixed_synop <- c("AAXX 01183 87736 12465 20000 10326 20215 39974 40064 5//// 60001 82100 333 56600 82818=",                  "AAXX 04033 87016 41460 83208 10200 20194 39712 40114 50003 70292 888// 333 56699 82810 88615="                  )  colorado_data <- show_synop_data(mixed_synop, wmo_identifier = '87736', remove_empty_cols = TRUE) #> Warning in show_synop_data(mixed_synop, wmo_identifier = "87736", #> remove_empty_cols = TRUE): 1 message(s) do not contain the identifier '87736' #> and will be discarded. knitr::kable(t(colorado_data)) library(synopR)  my_df <- data.frame(syn = c("AAXX 01183 87736 12465 20000 10326 20215 39974 40064 5//// 60001 82100 333 56600 82818=",                             "AAXX 01183 87736 12465 20000 10326 20215 39974 40064 5//// 60001 82100 333 56600 82818="),                     second_column = c(5,7))  check_synop(my_df) # Bien #> Error: #> ℹ In index: 1. #> Caused by error in `startsWith()`: #> ! non-character object(s)  check_synop(my_df$syn) # Mal #> # A tibble: 2 × 2 #>   is_valid error_log #>   <lgl>    <chr>     #> 1 TRUE     ""        #> 2 TRUE     "" library(synopR)  check_synop(c("AAXX 01183 87736 12465 20000 10326 20215 39974 40064 5//// 60001 82100 333 56600 82818=",               "AAXX 01183 87736 12465 20000 10326 20215 39974 40064 5//// 6000182100 333 56600 82818=",               "AAXX 01183 87736 12465 20000 10326 2021 39974 40064 5//// 60001 82100 333 56600 82818=",               "AAXX 01183 87736 12465 20000 10326 20215 39974 40064 5//// 60001 82100 333 56600 82818",               "Not a synop message=")) #> # A tibble: 5 × 2 #>   is_valid error_log                                               #>   <lgl>    <chr>                                                   #> 1 TRUE     ""                                                      #> 2 FALSE    "Invalid groups: 6000182100"                            #> 3 FALSE    "Invalid groups: 2021"                                  #> 4 FALSE    "Missing '=' terminator"                                #> 5 FALSE    "Missing AAXX | Invalid groups: Not, a, synop, message"

title: Introducción a synopR
text: Supongamos que necesitamos información meteorológica de la estación Rio Colorado (identificador WMO: 87736) para lo cual recurrimos Ogimet. Por simplicidad, sólo descargamos los SYNOP del 1 de febrero del 2026, utilizando este link. Podremos ver que los mensajes descargados desde Ogimet son SYNOP “puros”, sino que tienen un agregado, donde se indica la estación (87736) y la fecha y hora de la observación. Pero hay problema, porque la función parse_ogimet() fue especialmente diseñada para manejar estas situaciones.
code: library(synopR)  data_input <- data.frame(synops = c("87736,2026,02,01,03,00,AAXX 01034 87736 NIL=",                                     "87736,2026,02,01,06,00,AAXX 01064 87736 NIL=",                                     "87736,2026,02,01,09,00,AAXX 01094 87736 NIL=",                                     "87736,2026,02,01,12,00,AAXX 01123 87736 12965 31808 10240 20210 39992 40082 5//// 60104 82075 333 10282 20216 56055 82360=",                                     "87736,2026,02,01,15,00,AAXX 01154 87736 NIL=",                                     "87736,2026,02,01,18,00,AAXX 01183 87736 12465 20000 10326 20215 39974 40064 5//// 60001 82100 333 56600 82818=",                                     "87736,2026,02,01,21,00,AAXX 01214 87736 NIL="))  # Escribit `parse_ogimet(data_input)` es incorrecto! data_from_ogimet <- parse_ogimet(data_input$synops)   print(data_from_ogimet) #> # A tibble: 7 × 5 #>    Year Month Day_Ogimet Hour_Ogimet Raw_synop                                   #>   <dbl> <dbl>      <dbl>       <dbl> <chr>                                       #> 1  2026     2          1           3 AAXX 01034 87736 NIL=                       #> 2  2026     2          1           6 AAXX 01064 87736 NIL=                       #> 3  2026     2          1           9 AAXX 01094 87736 NIL=                       #> 4  2026     2          1          12 AAXX 01123 87736 12965 31808 10240 20210 3… #> 5  2026     2          1          15 AAXX 01154 87736 NIL=                       #> 6  2026     2          1          18 AAXX 01183 87736 12465 20000 10326 20215 3… #> 7  2026     2          1          21 AAXX 01214 87736 NIL=  # Se añade la columna 'Year' para el Año parse_ogimet(data_input$synops) |> show_synop_data(wmo_identifier = 87736, remove_empty_cols = TRUE) #> # A tibble: 7 × 25 #>   wmo_id  Year Month   Day  Hour Cloud_base_height Visibility Total_cloud_cover #>   <chr>  <dbl> <dbl> <dbl> <dbl>             <dbl>      <dbl>             <dbl> #> 1 87736   2026     2     1     3                NA         NA                NA #> 2 87736   2026     2     1     6                NA         NA                NA #> 3 87736   2026     2     1     9                NA         NA                NA #> 4 87736   2026     2     1    12                 9         65                 3 #> 5 87736   2026     2     1    15                NA         NA                NA #> 6 87736   2026     2     1    18                 4         65                 2 #> 7 87736   2026     2     1    21                NA         NA                NA #> # ℹ 17 more variables: Wind_direction <dbl>, Wind_speed <dbl>, #> #   Wind_speed_unit <chr>, Air_temperature <dbl>, Dew_point <dbl>, #> #   Relative_humidity <dbl>, Station_pressure <dbl>, MSLP_GH <dbl>, #> #   Precipitation_S1 <dbl>, Precip_period_S1 <dbl>, Cloud_amount_Nh <dbl>, #> #   Low_clouds_CL <dbl>, Medium_clouds_CM <dbl>, High_clouds_CH <dbl>, #> #   Max_temperature <dbl>, Min_temperature <dbl>, Cloud_drift_direction <chr>

title: Introducción a synopR
text: Que un SYNOP tenga una estructura válida significa que la información contenida en el mensaje sea correcta. El post-procesamiento de los datos y sus controles de calidad son tarea del usuario. El grupo 555, de difusión nacional, es ignorado, puesto que depende de cada país. Sin embargo, es posible que en futuras versiones de synopR se añadan funciones que permitan extraer datos de esta sección según las necesidades del usuario. hay soporte para las secciones 222 y 444. show_synop_data() incorrectamente decodificará estos mensajes.

title: Introducción a synopR
text: Los siguientes parámetros meteorológicos se decodifican completamente, debido que producirían vectores estrictamente numéricos, o que el resultado sería muy extenso: Visibilidad horizontal VV Altura de la base de la nube más baja h Cobertura nubosa N y Nh, pero pueden ser directamente interpretados en octas (octavos), salvo cuando adoptan un valor de 9, en cuyo caso significa que el cielo es visible debido la niebla o otro fenómeno meteorológico Tiempo presenta y pasado ww, W1, W2, descripción de las nubes Cl, Cm, Ch, descripción del suelo E y E' obstante, Tablas con códigos están disponibles (en inglés) en la sección “Code Tables” para conversiones directas! También deberías tener en cuenta que: Dirección del viento = 99 significa “dirección del viento variable” Velocidad del viento mayor 99 unidades (m/s o nudos) tienen soporte (en tales casos, el resultado final siempre será 99), pero es de esperarse que esto produzca un error Si el grupo 2 de la sección 1 informa la humedad relativa en lugar del punto de rocío, el valor final en la columna Dew_point será NA Para la altura geopotencial, solamente se aceptan los niveles de presión de 850, 700 y 500 hPa (para otro nivel, dará NA) Se ignorarn los grupos 5 y 9 de la sección 1 La precipitación imperceptible, codificada como 990, se la transforma en 0.01 (mm) La profundidad de la nieve, sss, se asume que tiene un valor entre 1 y 996 (cm). ‘997’ significa ‘menos de 0.5 cm’ Los grupos 5 (por ejemplo 55, 56, 57, etc…), 7, 8 y 9 de la sección 3 son ignorados

title: Tidy Decoding of SYNOP (Surface Synoptic Observations) Meteorological Messages
text: goal synopR provide simple robust tool decoding FM 12 SYNOP (Report surface observation fixed land station) messages, specifically optimized data retrieved Ogimet standard WMO formats. focuses extracting Sections 0, 1 3 tidy, analysis-ready format.

title: Tidy Decoding of SYNOP (Surface Synoptic Observations) Meteorological Messages
text: Install CRAN: install development version (0.3.0.9000) GitHub : Important note. development version differs stable CRAN version. former includes: new function, download_from_ogimet(). Optional parallelization speed check_synop() show_synop_data() (future package). show_synop_data() can decode lot information: 19 new columns related sunshine solar radiation, plus new columns cloud drift, pressure change precipitation last 24h. direct consequence support Section 3. direct stringr dependency erased. Therefore, recommend installation development version. estimated changes available CRAN version May 2026.
code: install.packages("synopR") # install.packages("devtools") devtools::install_github("ezequiel1593/synopR", build_vignettes = TRUE)

title: Tidy Decoding of SYNOP (Surface Synoptic Observations) Meteorological Messages
text: package extracts following meteorological parameters:

title: Tidy Decoding of SYNOP (Surface Synoptic Observations) Meteorological Messages
text: Clouds: Lower cloud base height (h), total coverage (N), low medium cloud amount (Nh), types (Cl, Cm, Ch). Visibility: Horizontal visibility (VV). Wind: Direction (dd) tens degree speed (ff) unit. Temperature humidity: Air temperature (TTT) dew point (TdTdTd) °C. Relative humidity derived . Pressure: Mean sea level pressure (PPPP) surface pressure (PPPP1) hPa. Also, geopotential height (hhh) gpm. Precipitation: Amount (RRR1) mm time window (tR1) hours. Weather: Present (ww) past weather (W1, W2).

title: Tidy Decoding of SYNOP (Surface Synoptic Observations) Meteorological Messages
text: Temperature extremes: Maximum (Tx) Minimum (Tn) temperature °C. Ground snow: State ground (E, E'), snow depth (sss) cm ground minimum temperature (TgTg) °C. Section 3 Precipitation: Amount (RRR3) mm time window (tR3) hours. Note: Parameters like h, VV,dd,ff, N, Nh, ww, W1, W2, E, E', Cl, Cm, Ch extracted original coded format WMO tables. N Nh, values 0-8 directly represent coverage oktas. Vectors WMO Code Tables available .

title: Tidy Decoding of SYNOP (Surface Synoptic Observations) Meteorological Messages
text: package includes parser specifically designed comma-separated format used Ogimet:
code: library(synopR)  # Example Ogimet string # Downloaded from: https://www.ogimet.com/cgi-bin/getsynop?block=87736&begin=202601011800&end=202601011800 raw_data <- "87736,2026,01,01,18,00,AAXX 01183 87736 11463 41813 10330 20148 39982 40072 5//// 60001 70700 83105 333 56600 83818="  # Parse and decode decoded <- parse_ogimet(raw_data) |>   show_synop_data(wmo_identifier = "87736")  print(decoded)

title: Tidy Decoding of SYNOP (Surface Synoptic Observations) Meteorological Messages
text: ensure accurate decoding, package assumes: Sections: package support sections 222 (Maritime data), 444 (Data clouds base station level) 555 (Data national use, automatically discarded). Timing: Observations assumed occur time indicated Section 0 (Group 9 Section 1 currently ignored). Wind: Speed (ff) reported m/s knots value exceed 99. Humidity: Group 2 (Section 1) contains dew point, relative humidity. Geopotential height: pressure levels 850, 700 500 hPa supported. Snow: Group 4 Section 3 contains snow depth value 1 cm 996 cm. Trace Precipitation: converted 0.01 (mm). Ground temperature: Group 3 Section 3 interpreted ground minimum temperature. Groups supported: Groups starting 5, 7, 8 9 Section 3 currently supported.

code: show_synop_data(data, wmo_identifier = NULL, remove_empty_cols = TRUE)

title: Decode multiple SYNOP messages from a single station — show_synop_data
text: data character vector, data frame tibble one column containing raw SYNOP strings. wmo_identifier 5-digit character string integer representing station WMO ID. NULL (default), messages decoded. remove_empty_cols Logical. columns containing NA values removed?

title: Decode multiple SYNOP messages from a single station — show_synop_data
text: tidy tibble row represents one observation time column decoded meteorological variable. wmo_id - WMO station identifier Year - (parse_ogimet()) Day - informed Section 0 Hour - informed Section 0 Cloud_base_height - Lowest cloud base height, decoded Visibility - decoded Total_cloud_cover - oktas, 9 means 'invisible' sky fog phenomenon Wind_direction - tens degree, 99 means 'variable wind direction' Wind_speed Wind_speed_unit - Either 'm/s' 'knots' Air_temperature - degrees Celsius Dew_point - degrees Celsius Relative_humidity - percentage Station_pressure - hPa MSLP_GH - Mean sea level pressure (hPa) geopotential height (gpm) Precipitation_S1 - mm Precip_period_S1 - hours ('Precipitation_S1' fell last 'Precip_period_S1' hours) Present_weather - decoded Past_weather1 - decoded Past_weather2 - decoded Cloud_amount_Nh - Cloud coverage low medium cloud, 'Total_cloud_cover' Low_clouds_CL - decoded Medium_clouds_CM - decoded High_clouds_CH - decoded Max_temperature - degrees Celsius Min_temperature - degrees Celsius Ground_state - decoded Ground_temperature - Integer, degrees Celsius Snow_ground_state - decoded Snow_depth - cm, assumed 1 996 cm Sunshine_daily - hours (generally previous civil day) Positive_Net_Rad_last_24h - J/cm^2 Negative_Net_Rad_last_24h - J/cm^2 Global_Solar_Rad_last_24h - J/cm^2 Diffused_Solar_Rad_last_24h - J/cm^2 Downward_LongWave_Rad_last_24h - J/cm^2 Upward_LongWave_Rad_last_24h - J/cm^2 ShortWave_Rad_last_24h - J/cm^2 Net_ShortWave_Rad_last_24h - J/cm^2 Direct_Solar_Rad_last_24h - J/cm^2 Sunshine_last_hour - hours Positive_Net_Rad_last_hour - kJ/m^2 Negative_Net_Rad_last_hour - kJ/m^2 Global_Solar_Rad_last_hour - kJ/m^2 Diffused_Solar_Rad_last_hour - kJ/m^2 Downward_LongWave_Rad_last_hour - kJ/m^2 Upward_LongWave_Rad_last_hour - kJ/m^2 ShortWave_Rad_last_hour - kJ/m^2 Net_ShortWave_Rad_last_hour - kJ/m^2 Direct_Solar_Rad_last_hour - kJ/m^2 Cloud_drift_direction - cardinal intercardinal directions "low - medium - high" clouds Pressure_change_last_24h - hPa Precipitation_S3 - mm Precip_period_S3 - hours ('Precipitation_S3' fell last 'Precip_period_S3' hours) Precipitation_last_24h - mm

title: Decode multiple SYNOP messages from a single station — show_synop_data
synopR 0.3.0.9000
text: New function download_from_ogimet() retrieve SYNOP messages Ogimet.com. data can obtained show_synop_data(): 19 related sunshine solar radiation, plus data cloud drift, pressure change precipitation last 24h. stringr longer direct dependency, favor R base, resulted improved speed (tested microbenchmark package). example, internal function responsible validating every single group now 10x faster. purrr dependency changed furrr. show_synop_data() check_synop() now compatible future ecosystem optional parallel processing. Argument “remove_empty_cols” default value show_synop_data() changed FALSE TRUE.

title: synopR 0.2.2
text: CRAN release: 2026-03-18 CRAN release argument “wmo_identifier” show_synop_data() now optional. Column “Sea_level_pressure” show_synop_data() changed “MSLP_GH” geopotential heights pressure levels 850, 700 500 hPa now supported Tests ~ 4000 SYNOP messages Argentina resulted improvement internal functions, now can better handle potential errors

title: synopR 0.2.0
text: Found SYNOP downloaded Ogimet end “==” instead “=”, now parse_ogimet() can fix . Also, show_synop_data() check_synop() now aware . Added new argument show_synop_data() named remove_empty_cols precisely removes empty columns. argument wmo_identifier show_synop_data() can either integer string. show_synop_data() generates two novel columns: ‘Wind_speed_unit’ ‘Relative_humidity’ (Magnus-Tetens Equation). Official WMO Tables conversion form vectors available. Fixed bug related internal function returning NULL string “=” character included input, instead removing . clear documentation.

title: synopR 0.1.0
text: Initial release.
